title: Storyworthy: Discover, Teach, and Tell Your Best Stories from Memoir to Marketing, from Personal Speaking to Executive Giving
author: Ken Daigneau
themes: Every story is about change — the arc from before to after
The Story Spine as a universal structural framework
How vivid narrative changes belief more than argument
Finding story-worthy moments in ordinary life
Character emotion arcs drive audience persuasion
Marketing narratives sell transformation, not products
The Before/After Bridge for pitches and presentations
The Dinner Test — authenticity over polish
excerpt: The most memorable communicators understand that story is not decoration — it is the vehicle through which belief travels. Storyworthy teaches anyone how to find, shape, and deliver stories that change minds.

relatedBooks.title: Made to Stick
relatedBooks.author: Chip Heath & Dan Heath
relatedBooks.reason: Explains why ideas survive; Storyworthy explains how to build the vehicles that carry them.

relatedBooks.title: The Hero with a Thousand Faces
relatedBooks.author: Joseph Campbell
relatedBooks.reason: The monomyth underpins the before/after arc that Storyworthy operationalises for business.

relatedBooks.title: Building a StoryBrand
relatedBooks.author: Donald Miller
relatedBooks.reason: Both use story structure for marketing; Storyworthy focuses on the speaker's own story as the persuasive engine.

relatedBooks.title: Talk Like TED
relatedBooks.author: Carmine Gallo
relatedBooks.reason: Direct companion for public-speaking application — Storyworthy gives the architecture, Talk Like TED shows the staging.

relatedBooks.title: On Writing Well
relatedBooks.author: William Zinsser
relatedBooks.reason: Classic on clear nonfiction craft; Storyworthy extends it with narrative structure and emotional arc.
